name: Pages
summary: The Pages class contains methods for manipulating a collection of Pages (Named “scenes” in the UI.) in a model. You get a handle to this collection by calling Model.pages.

methods.name: add_frame_change_observer
methods.summary: The add_frame_change_observer method is used to add a new frame change observer that is called with each frame of an animation, meaning the end user has clicked on a Scene tab (aka Page) inside SketchUp and the camera is animating to that scene.
methods.remarks: The add_frame_change_observer method is used to add a new frame change observer that is called with each frame of an animation, meaning the end user has clicked on a Scene tab (aka Page) inside SketchUp and the camera is animating to that scene. The method returns an integer id that can be stored and later used to remove the observer with the remove_frame_change_observer method.

params.name: object
params.doc: An object that implements the FrameChangeObserver#frameChange method.

returns.doc: A unique id of the observer

methods.name: remove_frame_change_observer
methods.summary: The remove_frame_change_observer method is used to remove a frame change observer
methods.remarks: The remove_frame_change_observer method is used to remove a frame change observer

params.name: observer_id
params.doc: The unique id returned by add_frame_change_observer

methods.summary: The [] method retrieves a page by either name or index.
methods.remarks: The [] method retrieves a page by either name or index.

params.name: index_or_name
params.doc: The index or the string name of the specific page.

returns.doc: page - a Page object if successful

methods.name: add
methods.signature: add(name = nil, flags = PAGE_USE_ALL, index = self.size) => Sketchup::Page
methods.summary: The #add method is used to add a new Page object to the collection.
methods.remarks: The #add method is used to add a new Page object to the collection. If no name is given, then a new name is generated using the default name for new Pages. If a name is given, then a new Page with that name is added. If the name is already used by another page, a unique name is created. If the flags parameter is given, it controls which properties are saved with the Page. See the Sketchup::Page#update method for a description of the flags that can be set. If index is given, it specifies the position in the page list that the new page is added. Otherwise the new page is added to the end.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of the specific page.

methods.name: add_matchphoto_page
methods.signature: add_matchphoto_page(image_filename, camera = nil, page_name = nil) => Sketchup::Page
methods.summary: The #add_matchphoto_page method is used to add a photomatch page to the model.
methods.remarks: The #add_matchphoto_page method is used to add a photomatch page to the model. This is an advanced feature that was added to support internal SketchUp work, so it is unlikely to be useful to you.

params.name: image_filename
params.doc: The image file to use as the background.

methods.name: add_observer
methods.summary: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the Pages object.
methods.remarks: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the Pages object. See the PagesObserver interface for more details.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: count
methods.summary: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module.
methods.remarks: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module. Prior to that the #count method is an alias for #length.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: The #each method is used to iterate through pages in the model.
methods.remarks: The #each method is used to iterate through pages in the model.

methods.name: erase
methods.summary: The #erase method is used to remove a page from the collection.
methods.remarks: The #erase method is used to remove a page from the collection.

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method is an alias for #size.
methods.remarks: The #length method is an alias for #size.

methods.name: parent
methods.summary: The parent method is used to determine the model for the Pages collection.
methods.remarks: The parent method is used to determine the model for the Pages collection.

returns.doc: model - the model that contains the pages if successful

methods.name: remove_observer
methods.summary: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.
methods.remarks: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object. See the PagesObserver interface for more details.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: reorder
methods.summary: The #reorder method is used to reorder an existing Sketchup::Page object inside collection.
methods.remarks: The #reorder method is used to reorder an existing Sketchup::Page object inside collection. new_index specifies the new position of the page. It should be a value between 0 and the max index of the Sketchup::Pages collection. Negative indices can be used to specify an index from the end of the list.

params.name: new_index
params.doc: Index of where to replace the page.

throws.when: if the given new_index is out of range.

methods.name: show_frame_at
methods.summary: Note: In versions prior to SketchUp 2019 this method will crash if called when there are no pages in the model.
methods.remarks: Note: In versions prior to SketchUp 2019 this method will crash if called when there are no pages in the model. The #show_frame_at method is used to show a frame in animation (of the slide show) at a given time in seconds.

returns.doc: Upon success it returns the page that is displayed and a parameter between 0.0 and 1.0 that is tells how far along the transition to next page you are at.

methods.name: size
methods.summary: The #size method is used to retrieve the number of pages.
methods.remarks: The #size method is used to retrieve the number of pages.

methods.name: slideshow_time
methods.summary: The slideshow_time method is used to get the amount of time that a slideshow of all of the pages will take.
methods.remarks: The slideshow_time method is used to get the amount of time that a slideshow of all of the pages will take. This takes into account the transition time for each Page and the amount of time that each Page is displayed.

methods.name: unique_name
methods.summary: The #unique_name method is used to generate a unique name for the page based on a base_name string.
methods.remarks: The #unique_name method is used to generate a unique name for the page based on a base_name string. For example, a base name of “Joe” might return “Joe 2” if “Joe” already exists.

params.name: base_name
params.doc: The base name to use for the unique name.

properties.name: selected_page
properties.summary: The selected_page method is used to retrieve the currently selected page.
properties.remarks: The selected_page method is used to retrieve the currently selected page.
